items.headline: Tech-Led Rebound Propels Nasdaq as Investors Eye Fed, Earnings
items.summary: U.S. equities are seeing a tech-driven rebound on Monday, July 6, with Nasdaq futures leading gains, while S&P 500 futures also rise. The market is stabilizing after a mixed previous week, with particular focus on upcoming Federal Reserve meeting minutes and the kickoff of Q2 earnings season. Oil prices are declining following an OPEC+ output increase. Not financial advice.

items.statContext: Building on last week's rally, S&P 500 futures were up by 0.44% to 0.45% in early trading.
Leading gains as tech stocks rebound, Nasdaq 100 futures saw an increase of 1.03% to 1.1%.
Slightly lower after reaching a record high last Thursday, Dow E-minis fell by 0.04% to 0.07%.

items.headline: Astera Labs Surges on Price Target Boost
items.summary: Astera Labs ($ALAB) climbed 10.5% in early trading after Bank of America raised its price target for the fabless semiconductor company.

items.headline: Western Digital Rises with Chip Rebound
items.summary: Western Digital Corp. ($WDC) gained 9.2% as chip stocks found their footing on Monday.

items.headline: AMD Climbs Amid Tech Rally
items.summary: Advanced Micro Devices ($AMD) saw an 8.5% increase in early trading, participating in the broader tech sector rebound.

items.headline: O'Reilly Automotive Retreats
items.summary: O'Reilly Automotive ($ORLY) fell 6.6% in early trading.

items.headline: Insulet Shares Drop
items.summary: Insulet ($PODD) shares were down 5.4% in early trading.

items.headline: Investors Await Federal Reserve Meeting Minutes This Week
items.summary: Market participants are keenly awaiting the minutes from the last Federal Reserve meeting, set to be released on Wednesday. These minutes are expected to offer further clues on the central bank's interest rate outlook following a cooler-than-expected June jobs report.

items.headline: Q2 Earnings Season Kicks Off with Major Reports
items.summary: The second-quarter earnings season begins in earnest this week, with key reports expected from major companies such as Delta Air Lines ($DAL), PepsiCo ($PEP), and JPMorgan Chase ($JPM). S&P 500 companies are projected to see a 24.4% year-over-year earnings growth for the quarter.

items.headline: Semiconductor Stocks Lead Tech Sector Rebound
items.summary: Chip stocks are showing a strong rebound today, driving the tech-heavy Nasdaq higher after recent weakness. The VanEck Semiconductor ETF ($SMH) was up 2.7% at the opening bell. Intel ($INTC) gained 3% premarket, while Western Digital ($WDC) and Advanced Micro Devices ($AMD) also saw significant increases.

items.headline: Oil Prices Decline as OPEC+ Increases Output
items.summary: Oil prices are falling on Monday after OPEC+ agreed to raise output targets for a fifth consecutive month. West Texas Intermediate fell 0.52% to $68.33 per barrel, and Brent slipped 0.50% to $71.76 per barrel, hovering near four-month lows.

items.headline: Microsoft Shares Dip Amid Reports of Job Cuts
items.summary: Microsoft ($MSFT) shares were down 1.7% following reports that the software giant is eliminating 4,800 jobs, or about 2.1% of its workforce, as part of its latest cost-cutting push.

items.headline: SK Hynix to Launch $28 Billion U.S. Listing Amid AI Boom
items.summary: South Korean chipmaker SK Hynix is set to launch a U.S. listing on Monday to raise approximately $28 billion, aiming to capitalize on the global artificial intelligence boom.
